examples: Not that long ago, a pukka British accent could at least get you a casting call as an army officer, a judge or Lawrence of Arabia.
The Bank of England has temporarily widened the range of securities it accepts to include less pukka bonds, such as asset-backed securities (ABS).
The first could be the case if the product you are selling to these "senior types" is quite pukka, meaning that a barrow boy isn't the right person to sell it.
He looks at Albert with the disdainful stare that the pukka sahib directs at a servant who has inconvenienced him.
